sections.paragraph:
εἰσπηδάω 1 aor. εἰσεπήδησα (s. πηδάω; Hdt. 4, 132 et al.; SIG 372, 9; POxy 37 I, 16; 1120, 14; Am 5:19; JosAs 5:1 cod. A; Jos., Ant. 5, 46 al.) a rapid motion forward into, leap in, rush in abs. (Menand., Sam. 564 S. [219 Kö.]; Herodian 7, 5, 3; PTebt 304, 10 [167/68 a.d.]; Sus 26 Theod.; TestJob 40:10; TestJos 8:3 πρός με) Ac 16:29. W. εἰς (X., An. 1, 5, 8; PHal 1, 169 [III b.c.]) 14:14 v.l.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

abs. abs. = absolute

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

PHal PHal = Dikaiomata: in a papyrus published by the Graeca Halensis—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 295
